rationale: The synthetic ensures has the shape `(big conjunction A) ==> det_equal(...)` — the conjuncts of A are NOT hypotheses in the body, they are the antecedent of the implication we must prove. The previous attempt asserted things like `post1.endpoint_dom() == pre.endpoint_dom()` unconditionally at the top, which failed because those facts live inside A. We fix this by wrapping all of the bridging proof work inside `if (A) { ... }`, so inside the branch A is a hypothesis and every per-conjunct bridging assertion (post1 ~ pre ~ post2) goes through, lining up the triggers and closing all the ensures-side equalities Verus needs.
